primary_reason: This systematic review evaluates the validity, reliability, and reporting biases of long-term-care quality indicators, including urinary tract infection indicators, providing a transferable framework for assessing infectious-outcome measures.
This hospital project combines a needs assessment, worker survey, stakeholder training, and local implementation to strengthen occupational infection prevention and control.

This study independently reviews sampled ICU records to estimate the sensitivity, specificity, and predictive values of a nosocomial-infection surveillance network and diagnose sources of reporting error.
This survey measures knowledge and attitudes toward adverse-event reporting among professionals involved in vaccination programs and identifies potential remedies for underreporting.
This study describes maternal deaths and their causes over a decade but does not identify a reusable infectious-disease design or measurement contribution.
This HIV study compares self-reported alcohol use with a plasma biomarker to detect underreported heavy consumption, demonstrating a transferable measurement-validation strategy.
This study reconstructs concurrent sexual partnerships from reported partnership dates and adjusts prevalence for possible underreporting to characterize an HIV-relevant network structure.
The decision-tree model compares vaccination strategies using cost-effectiveness and sensitivity analyses.
This multicountry pre-post analysis uses adjusted serotype-specific incidence to distinguish possible vaccine-driven pneumococcal replacement from secular trends.
This analysis combines recent-infection assays, multiple imputation, testing patterns, underreporting adjustments, and stratified extrapolation to estimate national HIV incidence.
This task-force report develops a definition and surveillance recommendations for atypical femoral fractures without establishing a concrete route to infectious-disease epidemiology.
This survey estimates needlestick injuries and reporting completeness among nurses and measures barriers affecting surveillance of occupational exposure to blood-borne infections.
This validation of self-reported body mass index concerns obesity surveillance and provides no concrete infectious-disease transfer route in the supplied abstract.

This study integrates laboratory surveillance, outbreak investigations, notifications, and regional survey data to adjust enteric-fever incidence for underreporting and describe antimicrobial resistance.
These two transfusion-related lung-injury case reports do not provide an infectious-disease outcome or a concrete transferable epidemiologic method.
This study links HIV cohorts to mortality records to quantify missing HIV mentions on death certificates and identify predictors of mortality underreporting.
This HIV cohort study compares physician-reported deaths with electronic vital-registry linkage and demonstrates the magnitude and correlates of incomplete manual ascertainment.
This applied pertussis study constructs and calibrates an individual-based stochastic transmission simulation to project the epidemiologic and economic effects of adolescent booster vaccination.
This polio analysis combines corrected observed incidence, projected program costs, and a dynamic transmission model of the no-eradication comparator to estimate long-term economic benefits.
This sleeping-sickness burden study combines hospital records, patient and health-service costs, DALY calculations, and a location-specific underreporting adjustment.
This blinded chart-review validation quantifies underreporting of central-line bloodstream infections and traces errors to specific surveillance-definition components.
This study integrates sentinel, treatment, and key-population data in EPP and Spectrum to produce national HIV prevalence, incidence, and treatment-need estimates for program planning.
This pair of one-day surveys reports healthcare-associated infection prevalence and ward variation without validating underreporting or contributing a distinct reusable method.
This longitudinal surveillance analysis evaluates Legionnaires' disease reporting and outbreak detection after diagnostic, notification, and prevention-system changes in France.
This qualitative study uses focus groups to identify knowledge, workflow, training, and feedback barriers affecting adverse-reaction surveillance in an antiretroviral program.
This review concerns non-steroidal anti-inflammatory drug hepatotoxicity and does not specify a concrete infectious-disease application or transfer route.
This review summarizes reported HIV prevalence and transmission patterns among men who have sex with men but does not substantively develop or compare surveillance methods.
This study evaluates an electronic immunization registry against linked birth records and household follow-up, quantifying coverage, missing doses, and duplicate records.
The title identifies an applied measles-outbreak investigation that specifically assesses substantial case underreporting in a defined region and period.
This study compares female sex workers' self-reported STI histories with biological testing and clinical examination to quantify discordance and its predictors.

This study develops participatory attitudinal ranking as a method for eliciting and comparing community norms about concurrent sexual partnerships when individual HIV-risk reports are socially biased.
This HIV/HCV cohort study compares alcohol reports from self-administered questionnaires and clinical interviews and models factors associated with interview underreporting.
This study validates a routine HIV clinic database against a research cohort and medical-record review, quantifying missing and incorrect opportunistic-infection and treatment data.
This review contrasts HIV case-reporting systems with population and community surveys and explains why survey-based measurement better captures burden under substantial underreporting.
This nested pharmacovigilance analysis uses reporting odds ratios in WHO VigiBase to detect disproportionate reporting of infections among users of DPP-4 inhibitors.
This dengue burden synthesis combines national surveillance, prospective cost studies, and empirical underreporting analyses to estimate societal economic impact.
This review describes Francisella infections, culture requirements, and aquaculture implications without contributing a transferable epidemiologic design or measurement method.
This study characterizes unreported tuberculosis cases and assesses how primary-care electronic records can improve case ascertainment and notification.
This article speculates about possible explanations for a low reported rate of neonatal group B streptococcal infection after waterbirth without presenting a systematic analysis or validated method.
This study examines appraisal of cognitive and functional deficits among people with HIV but does not measure infection, transmission, treatment adherence, or infectious surveillance.
This study links AIDS notification and mortality databases to quantify unreported AIDS cases and diagnose incompleteness in surveillance.
This vaccine-impact analysis shows how adjustment for underreporting, pre-vaccine trends, and serotype dynamics changes estimated effects of pneumococcal vaccination.
This pandemic-influenza burden analysis extrapolates laboratory-confirmed hospitalizations to the national population and corrects cases, admissions, and deaths for underreporting.
This retrospective tuberculosis case series describes presentation, diagnostic delay, treatment, and outcomes without a distinct reusable design or measurement contribution.
This veterinary pilot applies active record review alongside spontaneous reports to measure adverse events from antimicrobials and vaccines and expose severe pharmacovigilance underreporting.
This hospital questionnaire describes antibiotic-use frequencies and practice variation without evaluating a reusable surveillance, causal, or policy design.
This nationwide prospective study supplements vaccine-safety surveillance with a sampled underreporting assessment to estimate intussusception incidence and compare reduction procedures.
This study develops a joint maximum-likelihood estimator using registry and sampled hospital data to obtain corrected incidence estimates and confidence intervals under incomplete reporting.

This article prescribes multisource active surveillance, including clinical, primary-care, hospital, and occupational-health records, to correct underreporting of agricultural diseases that include infections.
This conference digest summarizes assorted dermatology presentations and provides no original or substantive infectious-disease method, diagnostic, or guidance contribution.
This study links Brazilian tuberculosis and AIDS notification records to recover unrecorded TB-AIDS comorbidity and calculate corrected state and national proportions.
This study compares health-insurance billing data with legally notified measles cases to quantify reporting completeness during outbreak and sporadic periods.
This qualitative study identifies deliberate gaming mechanisms and punitive incentives that produce extreme underreporting of hospital-acquired infections.
This single Rhodococcus equi bacteremia case highlights diagnostic difficulties but does not develop or evaluate a transferable epidemiologic or diagnostic method.
This review compares household, close-contact, and model-based estimates of influenza serial intervals and reproduction numbers and examines sensitivity to underreporting and generation-time choices.
This clinical review addresses anaphylaxis epidemiology and treatment without an infectious-disease outcome or a concrete infectious transfer route.
This equine-influenza outbreak analysis combines property attack risk, underreporting assessment, and movement risk assessment to characterize epidemic spread and response.
This investigation uses surveillance conducted during vaccination to estimate underreporting and correct morbidity estimates for an equine-influenza outbreak.
This study validates parent-reported adolescent vaccination histories against provider records and quantifies vaccine-specific false positives, false negatives, bias, and agreement.
This screening study reports hepatitis C prevalence, genotypes, and an underreporting percentage without explaining a transferable method for deriving or correcting that percentage.
This cross-sectional parasitology study reports infection prevalence, risk factors, and symptom recall using standard analyses without a distinct reusable methods contribution.
The study evaluates likelihood-based inference for detecting pathogen interactions under realistic simulated observation conditions.
The study audits mandatory MRSA reporting failures and proposes specific corrections to surveillance routing and data collection.
This population telephone survey applies a standardized gastroenteritis definition and compares community incidence with national reports to derive a surveillance underreporting factor.
This HIV-clinic study compares anonymous and confidential questionnaires to isolate how identifiability affects reporting of alcohol and drug use.
This systematic review synthesizes reported multifocal necrotizing-fasciitis cases, presentation timing, organisms, assessment tools, treatment, and outcomes to inform detection and management.
This systematic review quantifies underreporting of complications, including postoperative infection, and develops a reporting and management guideline for sacral nerve stimulation.
This analysis uses a national spontaneous-reporting system to characterize pediatric vaccine adverse events and changes in reporter groups over a decade.
This analysis shows that a national surgical registry undercaptures procedure-specific morbidity, including wound infection, and identifies registry modifications needed for valid outcome assessment.
This systematic review organizes occupational infection evidence into a matrix of professions and exposure pathways to identify hazards beyond diseases captured by routine reports.
This report describes the cases and control measures in a small rotavirus outbreak without a distinct transferable surveillance or analytic contribution.
This study synthesizes literature and surveillance data in a cost-of-illness model to estimate incidence and economic consequences of marine-borne infections and toxins.
This study compares patient-claims records with a national spine registry to quantify missing complications, including wound infections, and demonstrate the value of multisource ascertainment.
This applied study calibrates an age-structured deterministic pertussis-transmission model to corrected notification data and simulates alternative booster strategies under uncertain assumptions.
This study measures interviewer-body-mass effects on dietary self-report and provides no concrete route to infectious-disease epidemiology.
This One Health study uses nationally representative cluster sampling and parallel human and livestock serology to estimate brucellosis exposure and reveal clinical underreporting.
This study compares observed, prevalence-derived, and interval-censored regression estimates to correct maternal toxoplasmosis incidence for missed tests and diagnostic problems.

This standardized one-day hospital survey compares directly measured antimicrobial-resistant pathogen prevalence with national surveillance expectations and assesses the feasibility of repeated point-prevalence measurement.
This analysis combines dengue reports, underreporting estimates, viremia duration, inapparent-infection ratios, and statistical resampling to estimate viremic blood-donation prevalence.
This questionnaire describes physicians' hepatitis knowledge, vaccination, and needlestick reporting without validating reports or contributing a distinct reusable method.
This study compares deterministic and stochastic burden models and underreporting scenarios to assess how outcome variability, rare sequelae, and data quality affect pathogen-specific DALYs.
This longitudinal study compares reported sexual abstinence with repeatedly measured laboratory-confirmed STIs and identifies predictors of discordant behavior reports.
This hospital survey estimates occupation-specific needlestick incidence, reporting completeness, procedural knowledge, and barriers affecting surveillance of blood-borne pathogen exposures.
This typhoid outbreak investigation combines case definitions, cultures, molecular typing, surgical pathology, and a community survey to capture burden missed by routine detection.
This report describes early confirmed pandemic-influenza cases and only speculates that underreporting may explain low incidence.
This broad pharmacovigilance critique does not identify a vaccine, anti-infective, infectious outcome, or other concrete infectious-disease transfer route.
This multilingual review classifies post-Soviet infection-control systems by development stage, diagnoses surveillance and reporting weaknesses, and identifies priorities for reform.
This theoretical paper derives deterministic error behavior for duplicate and missing epidemic case reports and illustrates the resulting bounds numerically.
This news-media case review concerns deaths after cosmetic and bariatric medical tourism and does not establish an infectious outcome or concrete infectious-disease transfer route.
The study develops a preclinical mouse assay for diagnostic-antigen screening without transferable epidemiological measurement validation.
This statewide validation compares hospital-reported bloodstream infections with external chart review and adjudicates discordant cases to improve surveillance accuracy.
This review addresses hepatotoxicity from herbal products without an infectious-disease outcome or a concrete epidemiologic transfer route.
The prospective audit uses standardized follow-up ascertainment to identify underreported postpartum wound infections.
This qualitative study analyzes how gender relations shape men's STI testing and reporting practices, providing a transferable approach to studying socially structured surveillance gaps.
This review diagnoses underreporting, timeliness, age-coverage, and linkage limitations in Australian immunization registers and outlines improvements for vaccine surveillance.
This single lingual-tuberculosis case offers a differential-diagnosis reminder but does not develop or evaluate a transferable method.
This study compares a national patient registry with independently verified insurance claims to identify incomplete and inconsistent recording of post-arthroscopy septic arthritis.
This study tests whether falling reported central-line infection rates are explained by systematic reclassification as secondary bloodstream infections.
This Salmonella outbreak report links delayed clinical recognition and physician notification to delayed surveillance activation, illustrating an applied outbreak-detection failure pathway.
The study validates a multiplex PCR serotyping scheme and retains phenotypic testing to detect surveillance discrepancies.

This analysis jointly models clinical, virological, and serological influenza data to estimate reporting rates, reproduction ratios, subtype-specific susceptibility, and sensitivity to model assumptions.
This vaccine campaign establishes enhanced daily adverse-event surveillance, predefined event screening, severity classification, and expert causality review in a resource-limited setting.

This review explains diagnostic and case-detection failures in sleeping-sickness surveillance and prescribes active screening, molecular tools, reservoir control, and sustainable financing for elimination.
This study validates administrative MRSA coding through chart review and applies sensitivity corrections to estimate hospitalization trends that unadjusted discharge data miss.

This pharmacovigilance case-control analysis uses reporting odds ratios to screen antirheumatic drugs for disproportionate reports of hepatitis B reactivation while explicitly assessing spontaneous-reporting biases.
The study evaluates administrative claims as a supplemental source for correcting infectious-disease underreporting.
This study uses active gastroenteritis surveillance to estimate rotavirus underreporting and correct multiyear hospitalization incidence and seasonal bed-pressure estimates.
This study uses poison-center calls to predict methadone mortality but supplies no concrete route from overdose surveillance to infectious-disease epidemiology.
This paper uses antiretroviral-adherence trials to define standardized intervention-dose dimensions and reporting practices needed for valid dose-response synthesis and implementation.
This study links hospital discharge, laboratory, clinical-record, and statutory-notification data to quantify tuberculosis underreporting and identify its predictors over time.

This report enumerates neurologic visits in refugee camps, including a small number of infectious conditions, without validating suspected underreporting or contributing a reusable surveillance method.
The title indicates HIV prevalence and underreporting among men who have sex with men, but the absent abstract and letter format do not reveal whether it contains a responsible methods contribution.
This review concerns male sexual assault and forensic aftercare without an infectious outcome or a concrete infectious-disease methods transfer route.
This Rift Valley fever analysis applies epidemic curves, mapping, and a space-time K-function to test transmission hypotheses across multiple outbreak waves.

This multicountry study reconstructs pathogen-specific surveillance pyramids to adjust for healthcare-seeking, underdiagnosis, and underreporting and enable comparable gastroenteritis incidence estimates.
This applied dengue study estimates transmission parameters and simulates recurrent epidemics to compare modeled infections and interepidemic periods with reported Easter Island cases.
This review compares official echinococcosis notifications with clinical research counts to diagnose gross underreporting and inform future control surveillance.
This review addresses herbal and dietary supplement hepatotoxicity without an infectious-disease outcome or a concrete infectious transfer route.
This study identifies AIDS deaths misclassified to related causes from age-sex trends, redistributes ill-defined deaths, and corrects for mortality-system incompleteness.
This study uses first-line antituberculosis drug consumption and expected treatment duration to independently estimate treated cases, notification underreporting, and corrected incidence.
This study develops a two-stage seasonal time-series and Poisson-regression approach to attribute influenza-like illness consultations to pathogens and estimate their relative underreporting.
This annual report summarizes notified sexually transmitted infections and speculates about underreporting without providing a correction or reusable analytic design.
This study validates a childhood vaccination database through a survey of children lacking records and recalculates booster coverage using confirmed unregistered vaccinations.
This study retests clinically diagnosed malaria and typhoid patients with neutralization assays for four arboviruses to expose infectious co-infection, misdiagnosis, and surveillance gaps.
This news item has no abstract or supplied original method, diagnostic analysis, correction, or substantive guidance content.
This tobacco-control research agenda does not describe an infectious outcome or a concrete route by which its proposed surveillance studies transfer to infectious diseases.
The review identifies misclassification and underreporting mechanisms affecting maternal-sepsis mortality measurement and interpretation.
This accidental BCG needlestick case report describes a local reaction but adds no transferable surveillance, diagnostic, or correction method.
This mixed-methods study combines a multicenter survey and interviews to measure reporting of occupational blood-exposure injuries and identify modifiable reporting-system factors.
This evaluation compares clinically confirmed chronic hepatitis infections with a state registry to measure completeness of case, demographic, and risk-factor reporting.
This regional dengue burden analysis combines multiple surveillance sources, literature-derived expansion factors, systematic cost synthesis, regression imputation, and uncertainty analysis.
This systematic analysis derives and extrapolates empirical dengue expansion factors and evaluates their uncertainty through probabilistic sensitivity analysis.
This UK analysis combines routine and enhanced data sources to estimate the health burden of neglected zoonoses and identify underdiagnosis and underreporting gaps.
This study compares patient interviews with routine electronic antiretroviral records to quantify severe under-recording of characteristic treatment side effects.
This review synthesizes evidence and guidelines for pre-departure risk assessment and post-exposure prophylaxis after occupational exposure to blood-borne pathogens in resource-poor settings.
This questionnaire describes physicians' knowledge and self-reported notification behavior without validating reports or evaluating a reusable surveillance intervention.
This post-campaign cluster survey validates administrative yellow-fever vaccination coverage using self-reports, vaccination cards, age strata, and design-adjusted estimates.
This onsite validation uses interviews and retrospective chart review to quantify central-line infection underreporting and variation in surveillance-definition application.
This study tabulates reported pediatric vaccine adverse events by product and timing but neither measures actual underreporting nor contributes a distinct correction method.
This time-series policy evaluation compares voluntary and mandatory HIV notification periods using Poisson regression to estimate changes in reporting probability and missing data.
This paper integrates cognitive, economic, and statistical arguments to diagnose bias in human-adjudicated infection surveillance and justify objective automated case definitions.
This study validates self-initiated respiratory-infection reports against repeated recall questionnaires and assesses whether predictable false negatives can support corrected epidemic curves.
This protein NMR assignment study concerns a Coxiella virulence-related domain but does not contribute an epidemiologic design, surveillance measure, or population-level infectious-disease method.
This foodborne-illness model adjusts surveillance data for underreporting, underdiagnosis, domestic acquisition, and foodborne attribution and propagates uncertainty with Monte Carlo analysis.

This systematic literature search synthesizes complications of long-term circulatory-support devices, including clinically important infections, to assess morbidity potentially missed by usual reporting.
This rapid cryptosporidiosis investigation uses a cohort study and multivariable analysis to determine outbreak burden, identify a high-risk exposure, and guide measures limiting onward spread.
This comparison of prenatal cards with maternal recall evaluates general prenatal-care documentation and does not establish an infectious outcome or concrete infectious transfer route.
This study retests dengue-negative samples, demonstrates low NS1 assay sensitivity for dengue-4, and explains how secondary infections can cause surveillance underreporting.
This listeriosis study combines several information sources to estimate national incidence and compares the result with voluntary surveillance to demonstrate underreporting.

This study compares pressure-ulcer indicator reports with nursing records, but pressure ulcers rather than infections are the measured events and no concrete infectious-disease transfer route is supplied.
